title: Theory of Vigilance — Signal Strength & System Stability
description: Theory of Vigilance tools: signal strength computation (S = U × R × T), architectural stability shells (magic numbers), and epistemic trust scoring. Pure Rust via nexcore-tov.

tools.name: compute-signal-strength
tools.description: Calculate signal strength S = U × R × T from uniqueness (bits), recognition (0-1), and temporal recency (0-1). Returns strength in bits with interpretation.

tools.name: check-stability-shell
tools.description: Check if a system's complexity count is at a stability point (magic number: 2, 8, 20, 28, 50, 82, 126, 184, 258, 350). Returns distance to nearest stable configuration.

parameters.name: complexity
parameters.description: System complexity count (e.g., number of modules, tools, or components)

tools.name: score-epistemic-trust
tools.description: Score epistemic trust based on hierarchy level coverage (1-8) and number of independent evidence sources. Higher coverage and more sources yield higher trust.
